brief_document.title: Search attribute types, schema, and SearchAttributesUpdated event
brief_document.purpose: Define the core domain types for workflow visibility: search attribute type taxonomy, attribute schema registration, and the SearchAttributesUpdated event. These are the foundation the visibility store, engine integration, and SDK bindings build on.
brief_document.task: Add search attribute types to aion-core: SearchAttributeType enum (String, Int, Float, Bool, Datetime, KeywordList), SearchAttributeValue enum (typed values), SearchAttributeSchema (per-namespace registry of attribute name to type), and SearchAttributesUpdated event variant carrying a map of attribute name to SearchAttributeValue. Type mismatches on set are errors.

requirements.title: SearchAttributeType and SearchAttributeValue
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define SearchAttributeType with variants String, Int, Float, Bool, Datetime, KeywordList. THE SYSTEM SHALL define SearchAttributeValue as a typed enum with a variant per type carrying the concrete value.
acceptance: SearchAttributeValue has matching variants with concrete types (String, i64, f64, bool, DateTime<Utc>, Vec<String>).

requirements.title: SearchAttributeSchema with type registration and validation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define SearchAttributeSchema as a per-namespace registry mapping attribute names to their declared SearchAttributeType. Setting an attribute with a value that doesn't match its declared type SHALL return a typed error.
acceptance: SearchAttributeSchema::register(name, type) records the type for a name.
SearchAttributeSchema::validate(name, value) returns Ok if the value matches the declared type, Err otherwise.
Registering the same name with a different type returns an error.
Setting an unregistered attribute name returns an error.

requirements.title: SearchAttributesUpdated event variant
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL add SearchAttributesUpdated to the Event enum carrying an EventEnvelope, workflow_id, and attributes (HashMap<String, SearchAttributeValue>). All exhaustive matches on Event SHALL handle the new variant.
acceptance: Event::SearchAttributesUpdated exists with the specified fields.
The variant serializes and deserializes correctly.
All exhaustive matches in aion-core handle the new variant.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the VisibilityStore trait — that is AV-002.
SHALL NOT implement engine integration — that is AV-003.
SHALL NOT implement SDK bindings — that is AV-004.

constraints.text: VisibilityStore is a trait in aion-store, not coupled to any specific backend.
The libSQL implementation must support filtering by all standard fields and custom attribute predicates without full table scans.
Search attribute types are registered per namespace; type mismatches are errors, not silent coercions.
Visibility updates must not block the EventStore append path in server mode.
WorkflowSummary must include enough data for dashboard rendering without per-workflow history reads.
The search attribute event (SearchAttributesUpdated) is a recorded event subject to replay and determinism — it must be replayable.

resolved_context.intention: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running workflows of type Y. Without a query layer, the only access path is by WorkflowId — which requires knowing the ID up front. This cluster adds search attributes (user-defined key-value metadata on workflows) and a visibility index that supports filtered listing, full-text-like search on attributes, and time-range queries. This is the operational backbone that makes dashboards, alerting, and debugging possible at scale.

provenance.quote: Once an engine runs more than a handful of workflows, operators need to find them: show me all failed order-processing workflows from the last hour, find the workflow for customer X, list all running
